page: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 96 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 48 physical cores, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with 2.0 threads per core. The server includes 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and comes with one complimentary public IPv4 address. The CPU allocation is shared, which represents a potential performance tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, dedicated CPU execution.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10% at 213,580.47. Passmark CPU tests also show top-tier performance in extended instructions, encryption, and physics, all placing in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is more moderate; the stress-ng single-core test ranks in the top 25%, while single-threaded gzip compression is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is highly variable, with cached small-block reads reaching a top-tier 8,120.31 GB/sec, while uncached large-block reads and writes perform at average levels.

This server offers high resource density, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelizable workloads that can leverage the 96 shared vCPUs without requiring dedicated core guarantees. It is suitable for multi-threaded decompression tasks, as demonstrated by its strong bzip3 multi-threaded decompression score, as well as Redis deployments, where it achieves 101.76 million operations per second. Other appropriate workloads include static web serving, database operations, and moderate LLM inference tasks.
description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 96 shared vCPUs, 48 physical AMD EPYC cores, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by its stress-ng multi-core score and top 10% rankings in Passmark physics and encryption tests. However, its shared CPU allocation and weak single-threaded gzip compression present performance tradeoffs. Cached memory bandwidth is excellent at 8,120.31 GB/sec, though uncached large-block memory operations remain average. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded decompression, Redis database operations, static web serving, and moderate language model inference.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 96 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1280 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an AMD EPYC server with 96 shared vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1280 GB NVMe storage, optimized for multi-core workloads.
tagline: A ninety-six shared vCPU AMD EPYC server with local NVMe storage, optimized for highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 shared vCPUs and 48 physical AMD EPYC cores on x86_64 architecture
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, physics, and encryption benchmark performance
Variable memory bandwidth with top-tier cached reads but average uncached speeds
Best suited for multi-threaded decompression, Redis, and static web serving
